channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Seed Co Bets on AI and Climate-Smart Seeds @263Chat
item.description: By Tinomudaishe Muzanenhamo Seed Co says it is stepping up investment in research, artificial intelligence (AI) and regional production capacity as African agriculture faces mounting pressure from climate change, rising production costs and growing demand for certified seed. Spea #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Zimbabwe remembers Dr Joshua Nkomo 27 years after his passing @bulawayo24
item.description: CHIBWECHITEDZA, Mafukufuku, Umdala Wethu and Father Zimbabwe are some of the fondly referred to him. 27 years after his passing, the nation remembers the late Vice President Dr Joshua Mqabuko Nyongolo... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Ex-Harare boss files complaint with Zacc @bulawayo24
item.description: RETIRED City of Harare acting town clerk Phakamile Mabhena Moyo has approached the Zimbabwe Anti-Corruption Commission (Zacc) seeking an investigation into allegations that two fraud suspects delibera... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: China tightens grip on Zimbabwe lithium @bulawayo24
item.description: Chinese investors now control more than 80% of Zimbabwe’s lithium production, cementing Beijing’s dominance over one of the country’s most strategic mineral sectors even as a global price slump expose... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe grants duty relief for Mfecane 2.0 victims @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe has introduced a special customs relief package for citizens returning from South Africa, allowing them to bring personal belongings into the country duty-free and import business assets unde... #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Veteran ConCourt Lawyer Prof Loemore Madhuku reveals way forward as CAB 3 goes to President Mnangagwa
item.description: Inside the Legal Labyrinth of Zimbabwe’s Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 HARARE — In the dimly lit corridors of Zimbabwe’s Parliament, a legislative storm has reached its zenith, leaving the nation’s democratic foundations trembling. On a Tuesday (30 June 2026) that many legal experts are already calling a “dark day for constitutionalism,” the National Assembly [...] The post Veteran ConCourt Lawyer Prof Loemore Madhuku reveals way forward as CAB 3 goes to President Mnangagwa first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe steps up repatriation of nationals stranded in South Africa
item.description: Repatriation effort wholly funded by the government, Tagwirei pledge not received The post Zimbabwe steps up repatriation of nationals stranded in South Africa appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Bus Carrying Migrants for Repatriation Crashes Near Beitbridge @263Chat
item.description: By Tinomudaishe Muzanenhamo A bus transporting foreign nationals for repatriation has crashed near Beitbridge on South Africa’s border with Zimbabwe, killing the driver and injuring seven passengers. The accident happened on Tuesday as the bus was travelling from Durban to Musina #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: The Rapid Shift in Zimbabwean Sports Fandom and Digital Media @3menonaboat
item.description:
In recent seasons, the daily conversation has shifted decisively from television screens and physical stadiums to digital devices

The post The Rapid Shift in Zimbabwean Sports Fandom and Digital Media first appeared on Three Men On a Boat.

#Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews

item.title: From liberation hero to merchant of division: How Jacob Zuma is squandering the last remnants of his legacy @nehandaradio
item.description: As South Africa heads toward local elections, Gabriel Manyati examines why Jacob Zuma’s association with anti-immigrant politics could permanently reshape how history remembers the former president. The post From liberation hero to merchant of division: How Jacob Zuma is squandering the last remnants of his legacy appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Cross-Border Bus Fares Soar as Zimbabweans Flee Xenophobia in South Africa
item.description: Cross-Border Bus Fares Soar as Zimbabweans Flee Xenophobia in South Africa Thousands of Zimbabweans leaving South Africa amid growing anti-migrant tensions are facing another challenge, with some cross-border transport operators accused of sharply increasing fares and luggage charges to capitalise on the surge in demand. The spike in travel costs comes as thousands of people [...] The post Cross-Border Bus Fares Soar as Zimbabweans Flee Xenophobia in South Africa appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: DEADLY NEW TYPE OF CANCER: Karoi man loses his PEN!S after sleeping with married women, gets dumped by his wife and 4 children
item.description: A Life Undone: The Agony of Remigio Tom and Zimbabwe’s Battle with Belief and Disease Karoi – The story of Remigio Tom, a 47-year-old man from Karoi, is a harrowing tale that intertwines personal tragedy with deeply entrenched cultural beliefs and the stark realities of a devastating illness. His ordeal, which began with a mysterious [...] The post DEADLY NEW TYPE OF CANCER: Karoi man loses his PEN!S after sleeping with married women, gets dumped by his wife and 4 children first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Politburo meets today @zimsituation @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: Source: Politburo meets today – herald Advocate Jacob Mudenda Joseph Madzimure Zimpapers Politics Hub ZANU PF’s top leadership is set to convene a series of high-level meetings beginning today, with the party expected to review the implementation of key resolutions, assess its organisational state and finalise preparations for this year’s Annual National People’s Conference. The [...] The post Politburo meets today appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts @zimsituation
item.description: Source: SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts – herald Some of the buses deployed by the Government to ferry willing Zimbabweans back home from South Africa to their respective destinations in Beitbridge yesterday. – Picture: Edward Zvemisha Thupeyo Muleya and Ivan Zhakata GOVERNMENT has intensified the repatriation of Zimbabweans stranded in South Africa, deploying [...] The post SA crisis: Govt scales up repatriation efforts appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: MIDNIGHT DEATH: Police Commissioner Orders Probe as CID Detective Sergeant Milika Laison DIES Soon After Submitting Resignation @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Harare – The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) has launched a comprehensive investigation into the untimely death of Detective Sergeant Milika Laison, a 43-year-old CID officer, who reportedly perished in a road traffic accident on the Masvingo-Beitbridge Road shortly after tendering his resignation. The incident, which occurred on Friday, 25th June 2026, at approximately 10 PM, [...] The post MIDNIGHT DEATH: Police Commissioner Orders Probe as CID Detective Sergeant Milika Laison DIES Soon After Submitting Resignation first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
